text.title: gce-spot-vms Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gce-spot-vms as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using preemptible = true instead of provisioning_model = "SPOT" for new deployments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Preemptible VMs are a legacy product with a hard 24-hour runtime limit. Spot VMs are the current product, have no runtime limit, and support more features. All new Spot VM deployments must use provisioning_model = "SPOT" in the scheduling block.

failure-mode.description: Running Spot VMs without a shutdown script.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a shutdown script, all in-memory application state is lost when GCP preempts the instance. For batch workloads, this means replaying all work since the last checkpoint. For stateful services, it means data loss. The 30-second window is reliable enough to flush state to GCS for most workloads.

failure-mode.description: Using 100% Spot VMs for a production service with an SLO.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Zone-level capacity reclamation events can preempt all Spot VMs in a zone simultaneously. With 100% Spot and no standard VM floor, the service can go to zero capacity. Always maintain a standard VM baseline (at least 30% of min replicas on standard VMs) for SLO-bearing services.
